value: agent-harness-research: Key findings from researching Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Aider, OpenCode, Cline, Roo Code, SWE-agent, OpenHands (2026-03-07). (1) Aider repo map = gold standard for context packing: tree-sitter → dependency graph → PageRank → binary search to fit token budget. (2) Claude Code uses NO indexing, relies on agentic Glob→Grep→Read with 92% prefix cache reuse. (3) Edit format matters hugely: unified diff = 61% success vs search/replace = 20% on GPT-4 Turbo (Aider benchmark). (4) SWE-agent linter guardrail: reject invalid edits before applying = +3% resolve rate. (5) OpenCode: 1000-step hard limit + LSP diagnostics after every edit + snapshot rollback via git write-tree. (6) Ralph circuit breaker: 3 no-progress → OPEN → 30-min cooldown → HALF_OPEN → CLOSED. (7) Aider Architect mode: reasoning model proposes, editing model applies = 85% SWE-bench. (8) Roo Code Orchestrator: single agent switching modes (Architect→Code→Debug) instead of separate agents.

beads documentation follows 4-layer ownership: (1) bd prime is authoritative for live workflow commands and session protocols, (2) ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md has only machine-wide invariants (beads mandatory, no TodoWrite, use bd remember not MEMORY.md), (3) AGENTS.md has bd onboard snippet + repo-specific deltas (hardware testing, multi-agent coordination, recording decision tree), (4) CLAUDE.md has technical context only with pointer to AGENTS.md. Multi-agent primitives available in bd 0.58.0: swarm, slot, agent state, gate, merge-slot, audit.

The all_hardware feature lives on bin and driver-registry crates, NOT on server. CI feature-matrix.yml must use package: bin for all_hardware mock testing. Ring buffer benchmark runs twice in CI (workspace tests + dedicated step); the dedicated step uses continue-on-error:true since it's purely for metrics reporting.

Deploy scripts: deploy-maitai.sh (includes cargo clean), deploy-leabs.sh (NO cargo clean, must source cargo env for SSH). Both support --daemon-only, --gui-only, --skip-build, --branch flags. WASM build: trunk build --release in crates/ui, serve dist/ with python3 -m http.server 8080

Hardware-in-the-loop development cycle: 1) bd ready (find work) → 2) code changes → 3) deploy daemon (deploy-*.sh --daemon-only) → 4) navigate Chrome to WASM GUI → 5) connect to daemon, verify devices → 6) bd create --type=bug for issues found → 7) fix and re-test → 8) bd close, git push

Agent worktrees at .claude/worktrees/agent-*. Shared git objects — branches visible across worktrees. Clean up with git worktree remove <path> --force. Git config lock can occur during parallel creation — retry if needed. Use bd-safe.sh for worktree-safe beads commands.

Leabs-dev daemon at http://10.0.0.40:50051 (no Tailscale, local network only) has 3 devices: istar_camera (Andor iStar sCMOS), ipg_laser (IPG YLPP-200-1-50-R), power_meter (Thorlabs PM400). Use --hardware-config config/leabs_hardware.toml (mutually exclusive with --runtime-mode)

llama-cpp-autoparser: llama.cpp upgraded from b8179 to version 8231 (commit c024d8590) on all 3 vasp nodes on 2026-03-07. Includes autoparser refactor (PR #18675), XML argument reshuffle (PR #20171), true streaming (PR #20177). Qwen3-Coder XML tool calls now properly parsed into OpenAI JSON format. Chat format shown as 'peg-constructed' in logs. Build dir: /scratch/build/llama.cpp-autoparser-build/ on vasp-03. Binary: /usr/local/bin/llama-server-mmq. Old binary backed up as llama-server-mmq.b8179. Old libs at /usr/local/lib/backup-pre-autoparser/. NFS backup: /cluster/shared/llama-cpp/bin/autoparser-build/. SGLang NOT viable for V100S (requires sm_75+ FlashInfer, no expert-level MoE offload, no GGUF). Rebuild command: same cmake flags as before with GGML_CUDA_FORCE_MMQ=ON.

Maitai daemon at http://100.117.5.12:50051 has 12 devices: prime_bsi (PVCAM), photodiode+ni_daq_ao0+ni_daq_ao1 (Comedi), rotator_2/3/8 (ELL14), maitai (Ti:Sapph laser), power_meter (Newport 1830-C), esp300_axis1/2/3 (only axis1 connected)

driver-registry crate owns register_all_factories and create_registry_from_config. common crate owns DriverFactory trait (driver.rs:668), capability traits, Parameter<T>, DaqError. hardware crate owns DeviceRegistry (DashMap). All driver crates depend on common, NOT hardware (dependency inversion). plugin-api and plugin-example crates DELETED.

1340 cast warnings after changing lints to warn. Top hotspots: ui/image_viewer (80+77), roi_selector (61), driver-mock/pattern (58). 653 unsafe blocks total, 92% in FFI driver crates (justified). 51 blocks missing SAFETY comments (41 Comedi, 10 Andor).

cargo nextest run --workspace --profile ci runs ~2519 tests in ~35s. 2 known flaky tests: test_golden_startup_runtime_mode_universal, test_golden_mock_devices_registered. Pre-commit hooks: fmt, ast-grep, trailing whitespace, merge conflicts, private key detection.

Slint eval CONDITIONAL-GO: Slint 7.60/10 vs egui 8.55/10. E4 performance PASS: 1080p camera 163fps ceiling, bridge 134ns, idle CPU <0.1% vs egui 2-5%, WASM ~2.1MB gzip estimated. 3 conditions before migration: (1) gRPC-web runtime browser test, (2) floating-window UX lab validation, (3) license decision. Migration plan: 24 person-weeks 5 phases, MIGRATION_PLAN.md on eval/slint-gui-comparison branch.
WASM GUI served on maitai at http://100.117.5.12:8080 — Claude Code can interact with real hardware through Chrome browser automation (claude-in-chrome MCP)
